panels.description: Reed Richards and Sue Storm stand on a rooftop overlooking a cityscape while discussing the properties of a symbiote.
A close-up shot of Sue Storm looking concerned as she speaks to Reed Richards.
Reed Richards stands in a doorway, looking out at the city, while Sue stands behind him.
A large close-up of Reed Richards' face as he expresses a heavy realization.

text_blocks.text: ...IF THAT SYMBIOTE IS CAPABLE OF KEEPING THE INTEGRITY OF HIS MOLECULES...
...THEN IT SHOULD BE POSSIBLE TO REMOVE THE SUIT WITHOUT DESTABILIZING THEM AGAIN. IF I COULD JUST--
REED, YOU CAN FIGURE OUT HOW TO KEEP BEN HUMAN AFTER YOU SEPARATE THEM. BUT THE LONGER THAT THING STAYS ON HIM...
...WE DON'T KNOW WHAT IT'S CAPABLE OF.
text_blocks.speaker: Sue Storm

text_blocks.text: IT TAPS INTO ITS HOST'S SUBCONSCIOUS... THEIR DEEPEST DESIRES.
IF I TAKE AWAY THE SUIT, I TAKE AWAY THE THING THAT'S GOTTEN BEN THE CLOSEST HE'S EVER BEEN TO HAVING HIS DESIRE TO LOOK HUMAN BE FULFILLED SINCE THE ACCIDENT.
WITH THAT SUIT, BEN IS A THREAT TO HIMSELF AND OTHERS. IT IS OUR RESPONSIBILITY TO DEAL WITH THAT, EVEN IF BEN DOESN'T LIKE IT.
IT IS NOT OUR RESPONSIBILITY, SUE.
text_blocks.speaker: Sue Storm

text_blocks.text: IT IS MY RESPONSIBILITY TO BE A MONSTER...
text_blocks.speaker: Reed Richards

page_summary: Reed Richards and Sue Storm discuss the ethical dilemma of removing a symbiote from Ben Grimm. While Sue worries about the loss of Ben's ability to look human, Reed expresses a heavy sense of duty and the personal cost of his responsibility.
